item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image that was used in this article cannot be sourced, and thus it is to be assumed that it isn't cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0).

EDIT: The image has been sourced and is cc-compliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes. It’s an SCP that I may attempt to write one day, but currently is not on the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this. +1 from me. One thing that bugged me though is that there's repeated references to a blacked-out SCP that I think would have been better left as reference. Does that page just not exist?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aw, I like this one. The Tussie Mussie seems like it would be really useful, considering how many horrifying diseases (and Vector, if she got loose she could probably cause an extinction level event) the Foundation has stored. If the anemones and mandrake leaves show up everyone knows to get into the HAZMAT suits.

Flower language is really fascinating- not only was it common in the Victorian era, Japan had hanakotoba- same concept, but flowers meant different things. China, Vietnam, and various cultures in the Middle East also had flower languages. I guess people just like flowers as symbolism.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to TheeSherm from Site-42 for initial feedback, and Zyn for help in the Forums.

Edit_04DEC2019: For sourcing purposes, the picture was taken by me. :)
